description: Phase 3.2 cross-cutting manifest: attaches clause-level regulatory tags to existing UCs OUTSIDE cat-22 by creating minimal JSON sidecars under use-cases/cat-NN/uc-<id>.json. Each target UC already implements (via markdown SPL and narrative) a control that an auditor would recognise for the named regulation. Phase 3.2 closes tier-2 clause gaps for high-impact non-derivative frameworks (FedRAMP, SWIFT CSP, APRA CPS 234, HITRUST, NERC CIP, FDA Part 11, IEC 62443, CJIS, FISMA, MAS TRM, RBI Cyber, BAIT/KAIT, IT-SiG 2.0, HIPAA Privacy, SAMA CSF, SG PDPA, PSD2, ASD Essential Eight, UK Cyber Essentials, UK NIS, IT-Grundschutz, API RP 1164, TSA SD, HKMA, FCA SS1/21, PRA SS2/21, EU CRA, EU AI Act) while simultaneously reinforcing tier-1 coverage via cross-tagging. Derivative privacy regulations (UK GDPR, CCPA, LGPD, APPI, nFADP) are deliberately OUT OF SCOPE here; they are resolved in Phase 3.3 via the derives_from graph. No markdown is modified; no new UCs are authored.
out_of_scope: cat-22 UCs (already covered by Phase 1.3 migration and Phase 2.2/2.3/3.1 generators)
derivative privacy regulations (UK GDPR, CCPA, nFADP, LGPD, APPI) — deferred to Phase 3.3

mappings.assurance_rationale: HITRUST CSF v11 01.b User access management — detecting brute-force credential attacks evidences the authentication-control portion of user access management required for HITRUST certification.
CJIS v5.9.4 5.5.1 Access control — identification — the UC fires on repeated authentication failures against CJI-access accounts, the detection signal CJIS relies on to flag credential compromise.
UK Cyber Essentials Montpellier CE.SAU.1 Secure authentication & access — brute-force detection is the primary monitoring control listed under the authentication requirement.
PSD2 Art.97 Strong customer authentication — brute-force failure rates are one of the transaction-risk signals used to back SCA-invocation decisions.
HITRUST CSF v11 01.b User access management — tracking membership changes to privileged AD/Entra groups is the authoritative user-access-management evidence HITRUST assessors review.
FedRAMP Rev.5 Baselines AC-2 Account management — privileged group membership change detection is the core account-management monitoring FedRAMP 3PAOs verify.
ASD Essential Eight E8.05 Restrict administrative privileges — privileged group change monitoring is explicitly cited in the E8 maturity model as evidence of privilege-restriction enforcement.
BAIT/KAIT §5 Identity & access management — BaFin examiners expect documented monitoring of privileged group membership to evidence the IAM control.
BSI IT-Grundschutz 2023 Edition ORP.4 Identity & access — privileged group change tracking is listed as a mandatory identity-management monitoring control.
NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5 AC-2(7) Privileged user accounts — the UC is the standard detection for changes to privileged role assignments that this control enhancement requires.
PSD2 Art.97 Strong customer authentication — MFA challenge failure rate is the operational KPI EU payment supervisors use to evidence SCA is functioning.
UK Cyber Essentials Montpellier CE.SAU.1 Secure authentication & access — MFA failure rate monitoring evidences the multi-factor requirement added in the Montpellier update.
HITRUST CSF v11 01.b User access management — MFA trend data is one of several authentication-health indicators HITRUST assessors review.
ASD Essential Eight E8.05 Restrict administrative privileges — privileged session recording/audit is the Maturity Level 2+ evidence expected for this mitigation strategy.
FedRAMP Rev.5 Baselines AC-2 Account management — privileged session audit data feeds the account-usage monitoring 3PAOs sample during ATO review.
SAMA CSF v1.0 3.3.5 Security monitoring — Saudi Central Bank examiners cite privileged session audit as a core security-monitoring control for regulated FIs.
HITRUST CSF v11 01.b User access management — privileged session audit is one of the primary controls validated in HITRUST certification.
BAIT/KAIT §5 Identity & access management — privileged session review supports the IAM documentation package BaFin supervisors examine.
FedRAMP Rev.5 Baselines AC-2 Account management — automated provisioning/deprovisioning lifecycle evidence is precisely what AC-2(1) Automated system account management calls for.
BSI IT-Grundschutz 2023 Edition ORP.4 Identity & access — user lifecycle event logging fulfils the identity-lifecycle documentation mandate in this building block.
HITRUST CSF v11 01.b User access management — provisioning/deprovisioning audit is one of the baseline user-access-management evidence categories.
BAIT/KAIT §5 Identity & access management — documented joiner/mover/leaver evidence is mandatory for the BaFin IAM module.
SOX ITGC (PCAOB AS 2201) program-change and user-access ITGC — the UC evidences the user-provisioning subprocess auditors rely on when relating access controls to financial-reporting completeness.
FedRAMP Rev.5 Baselines AU-6 Audit review, analysis and reporting — Splunk _audit search activity provides the audit-review evidence demanded by FedRAMP AU-6.
SWIFT CSP CSCF v2025 6.4 Logging and monitoring — SWIFT CSP requires detection of anomalous searches/queries on the SIEM; the _audit-based UC is the canonical Splunk evidence.
HITRUST CSF v11 09.aa Audit logging — the UC audits the audit system itself, closing the HITRUST requirement for audit-log-viewer activity evidence.
SWIFT CSP CSCF v2025 6.4 Logging and monitoring — monitoring changes to the monitoring stack itself is explicitly a SWIFT CSP control; the Splunk conf-change UC is the direct evidence.
HITRUST CSF v11 09.aa Audit logging — tracking who changed audit configuration is a HITRUST-required component of the audit-log control.
NERC CIP-010-4 R1 Configuration change management — change tracking for monitoring-tool configuration evidences the change-management requirement that extends to EACMS.
BSI IT-Grundschutz 2023 OPS.1.1.2 Ordered ICT operation — configuration change audit is one of the ordered-operations evidence items.
FedRAMP Rev.5 Baselines AU-6 Audit review, analysis and reporting — audit-log modification evidence is cited in AU-6 when reviewing the integrity of the audit pipeline.
HITRUST CSF v11 09.aa Audit logging — the UC evidences audit-configuration integrity, a HITRUST-required subcontrol.
FedRAMP Rev.5 Baselines AC-2 Account management — Splunk role/capability change audit is the authoritative account-management evidence for the SIEM itself.
HITRUST CSF v11 01.b User access management — role/capability change audit covers the administrative-access-management portion of 01.b.
BAIT/KAIT §5 Identity & access management — monitoring-tool RBAC changes support the IAM audit trail required by BaFin.
FedRAMP Rev.5 Baselines SI-4 System monitoring — auditd rule violations are the canonical Linux system-monitoring signal for FedRAMP SI-4 evidence.
SWIFT CSP CSCF v2025 6.4 Logging and monitoring — host-level auditd rules are an explicit SWIFT CSP logging/monitoring control for the secure zone.
HITRUST CSF v11 09.aa Audit logging — auditd violation detection implements the OS-level audit-logging control in HITRUST.
FDA 21 CFR Part 11 §11.10(e) Audit trails — auditd violation detection is the standard Linux host evidence for the secure, computer-generated, time-stamped audit trail requirement.
EU AI Act Art.12 Record-keeping (logging) — host-level auditd monitoring records the infrastructure events that high-risk AI systems must log.
FedRAMP Rev.5 Baselines SI-4 System monitoring — privilege-escalation detection is called out explicitly as an SI-4(2) indicator of unauthorised use.
ASD Essential Eight E8.05 Restrict administrative privileges — privilege-escalation detection demonstrates the mitigation strategy is being continuously verified.
SAMA CSF v1.0 3.3.5 Security monitoring — privilege-escalation detection is a core Saudi Arabian FI monitoring control.
RBI Cyber Security Framework Annex-A Baseline cyber-security controls — host privilege-escalation detection is listed as a baseline control for scheduled commercial banks.
UK Cyber Essentials Montpellier CE.SAU.1 Secure authentication & access — password policy violation detection validates enforcement of the new authentication baseline.
HITRUST CSF v11 01.b User access management — password policy violation detection supplies evidence that the password sub-control is enforced.
CJIS v5.9.4 5.5.1 Access control — identification — the UC contributes password-related detection to the CJIS identification control.

FDA 21 CFR Part 11 §11.10(e) Audit trails — detecting changes to Windows audit policy is the direct evidence control for the ‘secure, computer-generated, time-stamped audit trails’ Part 11 requirement on validated systems.
SWIFT CSP CSCF v2025 6.4 Logging and monitoring — SWIFT CSP calls out detection of audit-policy modifications as a required logging-stack integrity signal.
HITRUST CSF v11 09.aa Audit logging — change monitoring for audit policy is an explicit sub-requirement of the HITRUST audit-logging control.

ucs.title: Process Creation with Command Line Auditing

TSA SD02C III.D Cybersecurity assessment — compliance check dashboards serve as the recurring cybersecurity assessment evidence.

NERC CIP-007-6 R4 Security event monitoring — the UC is a canonical BES Cyber Asset security event and satisfies monitoring-of-cyber-events expectations.
API RP 1164 3rd edition 5.3 Access control — unauthorized HMI access is listed as a primary detectable violation of the pipeline access-control control.
IEC 62443 FR 6.2 Continuous monitoring — register-value change tracking is the application-layer evidence of continuous monitoring on Modbus-based automation systems.
NERC CIP-010-4 R1 Configuration change management — Modbus register changes reflect control-system configuration adjustments that must be recorded under R1.
API RP 1164 3rd edition 6.2.1 Logging and monitoring — Modbus register tracking is an explicit operational technology logging control for pipeline SCADA.
API RP 1164 3rd edition 5.3 Access control — OT authentication failure detection evidences the pipeline access-control requirement on operator consoles.
NERC CIP-007-6 R4 Security event monitoring — authentication-failure detection is one of the canonical BES security-event monitoring signals required by R4.
IEC 62443 FR 6.2 Continuous monitoring — authentication-failure trending contributes to the continuous-monitoring Foundational Requirement.

ucs.title: IEC 62443 Zone and Conduit Compliance Monitoring

mappings.assurance_rationale: IEC 62443 FR 6.2 Continuous monitoring — zone-and-conduit compliance monitoring is the direct implementation of the FR 6.2 continuous-monitoring requirement for IACS.
NERC CIP-005-7 R1 Electronic security perimeter — zone/conduit monitoring aligns with the ESP boundary-monitoring requirement.

SAMA CSF v1.0 3.3.5 Security monitoring — VPN authentication failures are a core monitoring input for Saudi FIs.

IEC 62443 FR 6.2 Continuous monitoring — micro-segmentation audits feed the continuous-monitoring Foundational Requirement for control-system zones.

BSI IT-Grundschutz 2023 OPS.1.1.2 Ordered ICT operation — configuration-change detection is part of the ordered-ICT-operations safeguards.
SWIFT CSP CSCF v2025 1.1 SWIFT environment protection — network device configuration integrity is one of the protection signals the SWIFT attestation requires.
MAS TRM 2021 §4.1.1 Technology risk governance — change detection is listed as a governance KRI for Singaporean FIs.

API RP 1164 3rd edition 5.3 Access control — pipeline operators monitor SNMP authentication failures as evidence of the access-control posture.
ASD Essential Eight E8.08 Regular backups — tracking freshness of network device config backups evidences the regular-backup strategy applied to infrastructure.
NERC CIP-010-4 R1 Configuration change management — config backup freshness supports change-management recoverability for EACMS.
BAIT/KAIT §9 ICT operations management — BaFin expects evidence of continuous network-config backups in the ICT operations dossier.

ucs.title: Database User and Privilege Change Audit

mappings.assurance_rationale: HITRUST CSF v11 01.b User access management — database privilege-change audit is a cornerstone user-access-management evidence source.
FedRAMP Rev.5 Baselines AC-2 Account management — database user lifecycle audit is typical 3PAO evidence for AC-2.

HIPAA Privacy Rule §164.502(a) Uses and disclosures of PHI — database user audit limits who can access PHI, the minimum-necessary standard that underpins the Privacy Rule.
FDA 21 CFR Part 11 §11.10(d) System access limited to authorized individuals — database user/privilege audit is the primary evidence of continuous enforcement of the authorised-user control.
SOX ITGC privileged-access control — financial-database privilege audit is the canonical control tested for financial-reporting relevance by auditors.

Singapore PDPA 2020 amended §24 Protection obligation — DLP policy events evidence the organisation is protecting personal data from unauthorised disclosure.
Singapore PDPA 2020 amended §26A Data breach notification — DLP events are a primary input to the 72-hour breach-notification decision.
Singapore PDPA 2020 amended §26B Criteria for notifiability — DLP events supply the severity data required for the notifiability assessment.
HIPAA Privacy Rule §164.502(a) Uses and disclosures of PHI — SharePoint/OneDrive sharing audit evidences controls on PHI distribution through collaboration tools.
HIPAA Privacy Rule §164.514(a) De-identification of PHI — sharing audit supports governance on disclosure of identifiable health information.
Singapore PDPA 2020 amended §24 Protection obligation — collaboration-sharing audit is a direct control for the protection obligation on personal data.

MAS TRM 2021 §4.1.1 Technology risk governance — unauthorised change detection is listed as a governance KRI.
SOX ITGC change-authorization control — unauthorised change detection is the canonical program-change ITGC tested by SOX auditors to confirm changes were authorised before promotion to production.
FedRAMP Rev.5 Baselines SI-4 System monitoring — unauthorized cloud API call detection is expected CloudTrail-based evidence for SI-4.
FedRAMP Rev.5 Baselines AU-6 Audit review, analysis and reporting — the UC implements the audit-review automation expected for cloud control-plane logs.

FedRAMP Rev.5 Baselines AC-2 Account management — cloud IAM policy change detection is direct AC-2 evidence at the control-plane layer.
HITRUST CSF v11 01.b User access management — cloud IAM policy change audit is an expected user-access-management control.
CJIS v5.9.4 5.5.1 Access control — identification — IAM policy audit supports identification/authorization controls for CJI cloud deployments.
BAIT/KAIT §5 Identity & access management — cloud IAM policy change audit is expected evidence in BaFin cloud assessments.
PSD2 Art.97 Strong customer authentication — detecting admin logins without MFA evidences SCA enforcement on the payment-service back office.
UK Cyber Essentials Montpellier CE.SAU.1 Secure authentication & access — MFA enforcement on cloud consoles is one of the named authentication controls.
TSA SD02C III.A Cybersecurity plan — physical badge audit is one of the plan components when control rooms are in scope.
API RP 1164 3rd edition 5.3 Access control — pipeline-facility physical access-control audit is a named control in the recommended practice.
NERC CIP-005-7 R1 Electronic security perimeter — physical badge audit supports the Physical Security Perimeter requirement adjacent to the ESP.
HIPAA Privacy Rule §164.502(a) Uses and disclosures of PHI — physical access-control audit supports the minimum-necessary standard for PHI-storage facilities.
API RP 1164 3rd edition 5.3 Access control — physical access-control event audit is a listed control for pipeline facilities.
TSA SD02C III.A Cybersecurity plan — access-control events feed the cybersecurity plan when pipeline control rooms are in scope.
SWIFT CSP CSCF v2025 6.4 Logging and monitoring — after-hours physical access is one of the anomaly signals SWIFT users feed into monitoring.
TSA SD02C III.A Cybersecurity plan — after-hours detection supports the pipeline cybersecurity plan when control-room facilities are in scope.
ASD Essential Eight E8.06 Patch operating systems — container image scanning supplies the OS/container patch-evidence for the E8 patching strategy.
EU CRA Art.13 Obligations of manufacturers — image vulnerability scanning evidences vulnerability-handling obligations for product containers.
EU CRA Art.14 Reporting of actively exploited vulnerabilities — image scanning feeds the pipeline that detects actively exploited vulnerabilities in shipped container images.
